surface_verification.justification: Internal CI security-scanner producer (no web/CLI user surface). Verified end-to-end with live semgrep: a baseline --config auto scan of .github/ reproduced the exact 14 dependabot-missing-cooldown + 12 github-actions-mutable-action-tag findings; with the two accept env vars set both classes drop to 0 while an unpinned third-party action (evilcorp/thing@v1) stays flagged. Confirmed the owner is read from the file, not extra.lines (redacted to 'requires login' unauthenticated).

behaviors.behavior: Wholesale channel: an exact check_id in SHIPWRIGHT_SEMGREP_EXCLUDE_RULES is dropped from normalized findings
Owner-scoped channel: mutable-action-tag on GitHub-owned actions (actions/*, github/*) is dropped when the accept env is on
Supply-chain guard: an unpinned THIRD-PARTY mutable-action-tag stays flagged even with accepts on
behaviors.evidence: tests/test_semgrep_rule_tailoring.py::test_real_scan_shape_third_party_survives (line 7 kept) + live semgrep probe kept evilcorp/thing@v1

behaviors.behavior: Owner is read from the workflow FILE at path:line, not semgrep extra.lines (redacted to 'requires login' when unauthenticated as CI runs it)
behaviors.evidence: tests/test_semgrep_rule_tailoring.py::TestActionOwnerFromFile::test_reads_owner_from_file_line PASSED + live e2e: baseline 14+12 -> tailored 0 despite requires-login snippets

behaviors.behavior: Both channels default OFF -> normalize_tailored is a pure passthrough so adopted repos are unaffected
Invalid rule ids (path separator, glob, .., whitespace) are rejected from the exclude list
Fail-safe: unreadable/out-of-range file or no parseable uses line -> finding KEPT (never over-suppress)
Finding ids are renumbered contiguously after drops
oss_backend routes semgrep output through normalize_tailored so the CI self-scan honors the accepts
behaviors.evidence: plugins/shipwright-security/tests/test_oss_backend.py suite PASSED (439) + live _run_semgrep e2e on .github/ dropped both targeted classes to 0
